info.title: Controlled Document Templates Web Services
info.description: REST Services for managing the Controlled Document Template modeler.

get.summary: Retrieve the files meta-data for a given controlled document template.

parameters.name: $include
parameters.description:
Specify sub-resources that should be returned by the web request. Provide combination of comma separated entries including keywords none & all plus payload specific sub-resources:

[!]ownerInfo, [!]lockerInfo, [!]versions

Some entries are enabled by default which are described in the schema section of the response payload. For more information about refinement, see 6W REST Services Principles - Refinement Section.

get.summary: Search Controlled Document Templates for the provided input string.
get.description:
The search request must provide a string as input, and the service returns all the controlled document templates containing this string. The lookup for the string is performed on both the controlled document template meta-data as well as on the contents of the files checked in to the controlled document templates (anything that is indexed). You can use macros as $top and $skip to have pagination effect.The controlled document templates retrieved will search all index fields including file data provided to the searchStr=parameter. Maximum 50 controlled document templates are only allowed in a service call.Multiple criteria can be specified by adding + in searchStr. 
GET resources/v1/modeler/cdoc/templates/search?searchStr=TitleOfDocument+(current:Frozen)&$top=30&$skip=10

parameters.name: searchStr
parameters.description: Input Search Criteria; 2 or more characters required

parameters.name: $top
parameters.description: Represents the total number of items returned from the search, accepts a maximum value of 1000. The default value is 50

parameters.name: $skip
parameters.description: Represents the number of items to skip (to be used along with $top query parameter)

parameters.name: $include
parameters.description:
Specify sub-resources that should be returned by the web request. Provide combination of comma separated entries including keywords none & all plus payload specific sub-resources:

[!]ownerInfo, [!]originatorInfo, [!]periodicReview, [!]training, [!]AssociatedBookmarks, [!]sovaccess, [!]files, [!]subjectMatterExpert, [!]lockerInfo, [!]versions

ticketURL.description: The URL to retrieve the file from the FCS serrver.

ticketparamname.description: The Ticket parameter name required by the FCS server.

ticket.description: The ticket paramter value required by the FCS server.

fileName.description: The file name associated with the download ticket in the case one file is being downloaded.

fileNames.description: The file names associated with the download ticket in the case multiple files are being downloaded.

name.description: The name of the document object.

policy.description: The policy of the document object.

state.description: The current state of the document object.

isLatestRevision.description: The last revision number of the document object.

collabspace.description: The collaborative space of the document object.

modified.description: The last modified date of the document object.

hasReviseAccess.description: Whether the user has revise access to the document files.

hasDeleteAccess.description: Whether the user has delete access to the document files.

The Concurrent Engineering (CESTAMP) of the object; required for modify operations

updateAction.description: Specify the sub-resource update action. See 6W Web Service Principles: Data Object Updates

identifier.description: The unique object identifier when an object is represented by a Public Resource URI.

source.description: The object server URL when an object is represented by a Public Resource URI.

relativePath.description: The object resource path when an object is represented by a Public Resource URI.

csrf.description: CSRF object is required to be sent back for update operations and may be provided with read operations.

success.description: The response data when the request succeeds with 200 status code.
Reflects true to indicate successful request.

error-400.description: The response data when the request fails with 400 or 500 error.

error.description: An NLS error message.  In the case of a 400 error, the message is precise.  In the case of 500 error, the message is generic.

internalError.description: The technical reason of the failure - usually associated with a 500 error; this is not an NLS message and reflects the MQL or runtime exception.
